title: Psychotria niveobarbata
taxonomy: Psychotria niveobarbata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Psychotria niveobarbata being a part of the Ixoroideae subfamily. The Ixoroideae subfamily is one of the largest subfamilies within the Rubiaceae family and contains over 4,000 species. Psychotria niveobarbata is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria niveobarbata include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Psychotria niveobarbata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support overall health and well-being, as well as to treat specific health conditions such as fever and digestive issues.
